package service

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"net/http"
)

// Positive scenario for service_method_imports_http_status_or_abort_semantics:
// service depends on transport-specific status constants and abort helpers.
type SubscriptionService struct {
	repo SubscriptionRepository
}

type SubscriptionRepository interface {
	Create(ctx context.Context, userID, plan string) (*Subscription, error)
}

type Subscription struct {
	ID     string
	UserID string
	Plan   string
	Status string
}

func NewSubscriptionService(repo SubscriptionRepository) *SubscriptionService {
	return &SubscriptionService{repo: repo}
}

func (s *SubscriptionService) Subscribe(ctx context.Context, userID, plan string) (int, error) {
	if userID == "" {
		return http.StatusBadRequest, fmt.Errorf("user ID is required")
	}

	if plan == "" {
		return http.StatusBadRequest, fmt.Errorf("plan is required")
	}

	sub, err := s.repo.Create(ctx, userID, plan)
	if err != nil {
		return http.StatusInternalServerError, fmt.Errorf("failed to create subscription: %w", err)
	}

	_ = sub

	return http.StatusCreated, nil
}

func (s *SubscriptionService) ValidatePlan(plan string) int {
	validPlans := map[string]bool{"free": true, "pro": true, "enterprise": true}
	if !validPlans[plan] {
		return http.StatusUnprocessableEntity
	}
	return http.StatusOK
}
